description: Mechanism label corrections for long-form gold_standard entries

corrections.issue: 'humor' is not a mechanism, 'transformation' is vague
corrections.description: Staff in work clothes, hand covers camera, 'weekend clothes' text - reveal: same clothes. No one gets weekends.
corrections.reasoning: Setup creates expectation of casual clothes, reality subverts this

corrections.description: Stressed barista, kind customer says 'take your time', barista kisses camera (representing customer)
corrections.reasoning: Gratitude reaction is absurdly exaggerated beyond any realistic response

corrections.description: Four levels of chef pan-shaking skill, final level pan shakes itself magically
corrections.reasoning: Escalating skill levels culminate in physically impossible absurd finale

corrections.description: Customer orders julbord for 52 people day before Christmas with endless allergies
corrections.reasoning: Relatable service nightmare with escalating absurd demands

corrections.description: Customer on phone with earpiece, employee thinks customer is talking to them
corrections.reasoning: Classic misunderstanding from ambiguous context (earpiece not visible)

corrections.description: Cashier pretends to ask colleague for price, makes up high price, gives 'discounted' real price
corrections.reasoning: Fake negotiation/price reveal - the 'scam' is played for laughs

corrections.description: Customer takes too many anchovies during shortage, treated like criminal
corrections.reasoning: Mundane rule violation escalates to absurd criminal treatment

corrections.issue: Mechanism field got cut off mid-sentence
corrections.description: Workers dread announcing order 67, three kids go absolutely wild when called
corrections.reasoning: Dread build-up subverted by wholesome but chaotic reveal

overused_subversion_note: 24 entries use 'Subversion' - many are correct, but some may be using it as catch-all. Review needed: is 'Subversion' accurate or is there a more specific mechanism?
